evals.prompt: What are the exact CSS token names and hex values for all FDS warning state colors — text, background, border, and icon?
evals.expected_output: All four warning tokens listed with correct hex values from foundations.md and design-tokens.md

evals.prompt: I'm building a loyalty tier badge component. What are the correct FDS background and text color tokens for all four loyalty tiers — Burgundy, Silver, Gold, and Platinum?
evals.expected_output: All 8 tokens (bg + text × 4 tiers) with correct hex values

evals.prompt: What font family, size, and weight does FDS specify for a page title, body text, and caption? Give me the base type tokens and the brand role they map to.
evals.expected_output: Jotia for headings, Graphik for body; correct sizes from the 11-step type scale; brand role mapping

evals.prompt: Show me how to apply FDS typography tokens correctly to an h1 heading and a paragraph. What composite token names do I use, and why should I never set font-size individually?
evals.expected_output: Uses font: var(--f-brand-type-*) composite shorthand; explains why individual properties break the system

evals.prompt: List all FDS spacing tokens from smallest to largest with their pixel values. Which brand aliases should I use in code?
evals.expected_output: Full spacing scale from --f-brand-space-2xs (4px) through --f-brand-space-6xl (80px) with brand aliases

evals.prompt: I'm adding a hover transition to an FDS button. What motion duration and easing tokens should I use? What are the actual millisecond values for each duration?
evals.expected_output: instant=240ms, quick=480ms, gentle=960ms, generous=1200ms; correct easing token names

evals.prompt: I need three FDS icons: one for luggage, one for a Visa payment method, and one Arabic RTL variant of the cart icon. What are the correct icon names, prefixes, and sizes?
evals.expected_output: ic_nav_luggage (24px), ic_payment_visa (48px), ic_nav_ar_cart (24px) or equivalent correct names

evals.prompt: What FDS shadow token should I use for a modal dialog versus a dropdown tooltip? What are the actual CSS values behind those tokens?
evals.expected_output: --f-brand-shadow-large for modal, --f-brand-shadow-medium for dropdown; correct shadow values

evals.prompt: What FDS border radius token gives a pill-shaped button, and what pixel value does it resolve to? Also what token gives the standard button/input corner rounding?
evals.expected_output: --f-brand-radius-rounded (64px) for pill; --f-brand-radius-base (8px) for standard

evals.prompt: Build a complete FDS-compliant primary button and secondary button in HTML and CSS. Include the token map on the block, all interactive states (hover, focus-visible, disabled), and correct BEM class naming.
evals.expected_output: .f-button block with token map, .f-button--secondary modifier, all states using component tokens, composite font shorthand

evals.prompt: How do I build an FDS text input field with default, focused, error, and disabled states? Show me the correct HTML structure, BEM naming, and all CSS tokens for each state.
evals.expected_output: .f-input BEM structure, token map on block, all 4 states using brand tokens, correct border and focus tokens

evals.prompt: I need to implement an FDS flight status badge that shows Confirmed, Delayed, and Cancelled states. Use the correct color tokens, icon names, and BEM structure.
evals.expected_output: --f-brand-color-flight-status-confirmed (#34DB80), delayed (#FCC500), cancelled (#D95757); ic_flightindicator_ icons

evals.prompt: Explain the FDS three-tier token system. Show me a concrete example of how base, brand, and component tokens chain together for a navigation link's active color.
evals.expected_output: Base → brand → component chain explained; example shows why you never use base tokens in components

evals.prompt: Review this CSS for FDS compliance: `.search-bar { background: #F2F3FA; font-size: 16px; color: #1F212B; border: 1px solid #DBDEE8; border-radius: 8px; padding: 12px 16px; }` — list every violation and show the corrected version.
evals.expected_output: Identifies: hardcoded hex (4 violations), font-size not using composite shorthand, 1px not tokenised, 8px not tokenised, 12px/16px not tokenised

evals.prompt: I'm building a flight search form in FDS. What layout principles, field ordering rules, spacing guidelines, and validation behavior should I follow?
evals.expected_output: Single-column layout, labels above inputs, validate on blur, FDS spacing tokens, submit at bottom

evals.prompt: What grid system and breakpoints does FDS define? How many columns at mobile, tablet, and desktop? What breakpoint token values should I use?
evals.expected_output: 4 cols at xs-sm, 8 cols at tablet, 12 cols at desktop; correct breakpoint token values from foundations.md

evals.prompt: Write three FDS-compliant error messages: (1) a payment that couldn't be processed, (2) a passport photo that's too blurry to scan, (3) a selected seat that's just been taken by another passenger.
evals.expected_output: Sentence case, active voice, empathetic + solution-focused tone matching FDS content guidelines

evals.prompt: What are the FDS rules for UI copy casing, voice, and tone? Give me five examples of incorrect button labels and their corrected FDS-compliant versions.
evals.expected_output: Sentence case rule; active voice; warm/confident/premium tone; 5 before/after examples

evals.prompt: I need to build an FDS card component that supports both English LTR and Arabic RTL layouts. What icon variants do I need, what fonts change, what Figma prop controls the language switch, and what CSS considerations apply?
evals.expected_output: ic_nav_ar_ prefix for RTL icons; Almarai font for Arabic; Language: English/Arabic Figma prop; dir=rtl CSS

evals.prompt: Build me a complete FDS QSuite tag component — the kind shown on flight search results to mark Qatar Airways' premium cabin. Include the correct color token, BEM structure, appropriate icon or label, and all interactive/display states.
evals.expected_output: --f-brand-color-tags-qsuite (#8E2157); correct BEM .f-tag block; appropriate token map; luxury/premium visual treatment
